subject: Product Management
title: Communicating the Roadmap
description: A micro-lesson that makes Communicating the Roadmap usable.

hook.text: Different audiences need different roadmaps. The CEO sees strategy. The team sees details. Customers see outcomes.

buildup.text: A common mistake: sharing the engineering backlog as the roadmap. It's too detailed for executives and too vague for engineers.

discovery.text: Effective roadmap communication uses three layers: vision (why), strategy (themes), and execution (what's next).

twist.text: Never put dates on a public roadmap unless you're willing to bet your reputation. 'Coming in Q3' becomes a binding promise.

climax.text: The best roadmap communication tool is a regular cadence — monthly updates that build trust through consistency and transparency.

punchline.text: Same roadmap, different zoom levels. Match the audience.

quiz.question: How should roadmaps be communicated to different audiences?
options: Use different zoom levels — vision for executives, details for teams, outcomes for customers
Share the same detailed backlog with everyone
Never share the roadmap outside the product team
